content.problemStatement:
Nice work on building your first project using Java. We have curated many more projects for you to build.

Note that this is a beta course and we would love to hear any feedback on how we can improve this project, content or anything else.

Please use the star button on the right hand side to provide feedback.

And then, let's build a few more (and bigger) projects.
constraints: S consists of printable ASCII characters
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 10), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a string S (0 ≤ |S| ≤ 100) which may include spaces and any printable ASCII characters.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single integer on its own line – the number of vowels (a, e, i, o, u, case‑insensitive) present in the corresponding string.

sampleTestCases.input:
3
Hello World
AEIOU
a bc d
sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: "Hello World" contains vowels e, o, o → 3. Test case 2: "AEIOU" contains all five vowels → 5. Test case 3: "a bc d" contains only the vowel a → 1.
First string is empty → 0 vowels. Second string "xyz" has no vowels → 0.
The string has vowels: u, e, e, a, a, e → 6.

solution.approach: For each test case we read the whole line as a string. We iterate over every character, convert it to lower‑case and check whether it is one of the five vowel letters a, e, i, o, u. If it is, we increment a counter. After scanning the whole line we output the counter. The algorithm is linear in the length of the string and uses O(1) extra memory.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * L) where L is the length of each string (at most 100).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) additional space besides the input string.
